demographics.audit_notes: Corrected total_population from 1250. The original value was implausibly low for a neighborhood under active development, given the 1990 census tract population of 3,755. The new value is an estimate based on development timelines and provides a more realistic baseline for the rapid growth during the 1990s.
Corrected total_population from 1850. Value is linearly interpolated between corrected 1990 baseline and confirmed 2000 census data. Original value showed an implausible 48% YoY growth.
Corrected total_population from 2600. Value is linearly interpolated between corrected 1990 baseline and confirmed 2000 census data.
Corrected total_population from 3400. Value is linearly interpolated between corrected 1990 baseline and confirmed 2000 census data.
Corrected total_population from 4250. Value is linearly interpolated between corrected 1990 baseline and confirmed 2000 census data.

Corrected total_population from 6700. Value is linearly interpolated between corrected 1990 baseline and confirmed 2000 census data.
Corrected total_population from 6950. Value is linearly interpolated between corrected 1990 baseline and confirmed 2000 census data.
All original values align with 2000 Decennial Census data for Tract 17.18. This confirms the neighborhood was largely built-out and populated by this time.
First year of ACS data availability; new fields are based on the earliest ACS 5-year estimates.
Values align with 2010 Census and early ACS data for the newly defined Tract 17.31. The neighborhood is now demographically stable and aging.
Values align with 2020 Census and ACS data. Continued trend of aging population and high Asian demographic share.
Post-2020 data is projected based on established trends.
